title: Roman chamomile and oat melt-and-pour soap
subtitle: A gentle, soothing bar with colloidal oatmeal and roman chamomile essential oil in an opaque glycerine base
excerpt: An opaque white melt-and-pour base carries colloidal oatmeal and roman chamomile essential oil into a bar suited to sensitive skin. No lye handling required. The oat adds a gentle exfoliating quality; the chamomile a soft, apple-like sweetness. Ready to use after 4 hours.
sourceNotes: Melt-and-pour bases: The Soapmaker's Companion, Cavitch (1997). Colloidal oatmeal in cosmetics: FDA monograph for colloidal oatmeal as a skin protectant (21 CFR 347). Roman chamomile (Anthemis nobilis) EO usage in rinse-off soap: IFRA standard; usage rate up to 3% for rinse-off products is considered safe for most skin types. Contains gluten (oatmeal).

recipe.batchNotes: Double the batch for a full loaf mould. Proportions hold at double.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use after 4 hours. Wrap in cling film for storage.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months wrapped in cling film or in an airtight container. Glycerine bases attract surface moisture in humid rooms. Discard if mould appears or if the bar develops an off smell.

content.text: Colloidal oatmeal has a long record in soothing skin preparations: the milled oat creates a slightly silky feel in the lather and a very light exfoliation that does not scratch. Combined with roman chamomile essential oil, which has a mild, apple-sweet character and a reputation for calming reactive skin, this melt-and-pour bar is well suited to normal to sensitive skin types. The opaque white glycerine base hides the oat particles and gives a clean, cream-coloured bar. No lye is involved; the base is pre-made and ready to melt.

items.prepNote: Opaque white melt-and-pour glycerine soap base
Finely milled cosmetic grade; contains gluten
About 1% of base weight; added when the base has cooled to 55°C

content.text: Cut the melt-and-pour base into small pieces. Melt over a double boiler, stirring occasionally, until fully liquid. Keep the temperature between 55 and 65°C. Do not boil. Remove from heat once melted.
Add the oatmeal and essential oil

Allow the melted base to cool to around 55°C. Stir in the colloidal oatmeal, distributing it evenly throughout the base. Add the roman chamomile essential oil and stir gently; avoid whisking in air bubbles.
Pour the base into the silicone mould. Spritz the surface with isopropyl alcohol to break surface bubbles. Leave to set at room temperature for at least 4 hours.
Flex the silicone mould to release. Cut into bars if using a loaf mould. Wrap each bar in cling film immediately to prevent the glycerine base from pulling moisture from the air.

Wrapped bars keep 12 months in a cool, dry place. Unwrapped bars in a bathroom develop surface moisture beads (glycerine dew) in humid conditions; this is harmless. Discard if mould appears or if the bar develops a sour or off smell. Patch test before use if skin is reactive.
